text.title: Single Channel Bet Selector Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that turn channel-picking from intuition into a written, falsifiable decision a solo founder can commit to for 90 days.

rule.statement: The selection MUST be a written rubric (single page, version-controlled or dated doc) listing every candidate channel scored on three named axes; verbal "I think SEO feels right" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Solo founders fail by trying four channels poorly; the failure mode is invisible because each pivot is justified verbally. A written rubric makes the decision falsifiable and re-readable in 90 days when the bet is graded.

rule.statement: Each candidate channel MUST be scored on three axes: founder_fit (1–5, will you actually do it weekly without quitting), ICP_density (1–5, are 50%+ of your customers already there), early_traction (1–5, have you had at least one inbound lead from this channel unsolicited).
rule.rationale: Channels list methodologies enumerate options but do not help pick the ONE that fits founder DNA + ICP. The three-axis intersection forces alignment between what you'll execute, who you serve, and what already works — failing any one axis is a leading indicator of 90-day failure.

example.code: | Channel | Founder fit | ICP density | Early traction | Total | |---------|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------| | SEO | 2 | 5 | 4 | 11 | | LinkedIn| 5 | 4 | 4 | 13 ← | | Cold email | 1 | 3 | 1 | 5 | | Podcast tour | 3 | 4 | 2 | 9 |

rule.statement: The chosen channel MUST score ≥12/15 total AND ≥3/5 on every individual axis; channels below the floor are rejected even if total is high.
rule.rationale: A channel that scores 5/5/2 (no early traction) burns 90 days of execution to discover what the rubric already said. The per-axis floor prevents the spreadsheet from rationalising a known-bad bet.

rule.statement: The founder MUST commit to exactly ONE channel for 90 days and document this commitment alongside the rubric; channels 2 and 3 are explicitly parked, not pursued in parallel.
rule.rationale: The whole point is that solo founders fail by trying 4 channels poorly. Parallel pursuit destroys the per-channel signal-to-noise ratio and means none of them gets enough reps to compound. Writing down the parking lot makes the trade-off visible and reversible at the 90-day grade.

rule.statement: The plan MUST name a written 90-day kill criterion (e.g. "if I have not produced X leads / Y signups by week 12, the bet failed and I re-run the rubric").
rule.rationale: Without a kill criterion, the bet has no exit and either drags on indefinitely or gets abandoned secretly. An explicit pre-committed metric removes the founder's emotional sunk-cost vote at week 12.

example.code: Kill criterion (set 2026-05-20): If LinkedIn posts have not produced ≥10 qualified demo requests AND ≥1 paying customer by 2026-08-20, the bet has failed. On that date I re-score the rubric and pick a new single channel.
